1 / 13
Faber2026 · group-meeting update · 2026-07-17

Joint time–frequency fits, Phase A: 9 of 12 bursts

Simultaneous CHIME (400–800 MHz) + DSA-110 (1.28–1.53 GHz) burst-model fits with the amended windows and resolutions, evidence-selected component counts, and post-fit residual gates.

  • Done: 9 fits converged on h17 (dynesty, nlive = 400, run to dlogz < 0.5), each with a data/model/residual triptych and an automated residual verdict.
  • Still running: phineas (C3D3), zach (C2D3), hamilton (C4D1) — the three highest-complexity models, ~5 h elapsed at review time.
  • Why this campaign exists: the previous joint fits used hand-set, sometimes too-narrow windows (CHIME data hatched away that exists at DSA), unjustified downsampling, and in at least two bursts (whitney, zach) ignored a second burst component outright.
  • Verdict legend: CLEAN — no residual structure survives the gates; SHAPE — a ± dipole: the model component's shape is wrong, but no component is missing; ESCALATE — ≥5σ contiguous positive residual: the model is missing emission.
2 / 13
Method

What the model is, and how to read a triptych

The joint model

Each burst is a set of components per band (CnDm = n CHIME + m DSA components), each with arrival time t₀, width ζ, and amplitude spectrum, tied together by one scattering law τ(ν) = τ1GHzν−α, one screen spectral index β, and a per-band DM offset ΔDM. Sampled with dynesty; medians ± 16/84th percentiles.

Triptych anatomy

Columns: Data | Model | Residual/σ. Both bands share the time axis — DSA on top (1.3–1.5 GHz), CHIME below (400–800 MHz), hatching = no coverage. Top strip: band-integrated profiles (black = CHIME, red = DSA). The residual panel is in units of the off-pulse noise, so color saturation is significance.

What changed vs the old fits

Windows are now the robust common on-pulse span (5σ core grown to 1.5σ tails, both bands — no more one-sided hatching), and time/frequency downsampling is S/N-driven (target S/N ≈ 10 per resolution element) instead of ad hoc. Component counts are hypotheses to be tested, not assumptions: every fit passes a residual gate afterward.

Known limits going in

Evidence values with profiled gain-variance are not comparable across component counts (ADR-0003), so count selection uses fixed-s² ladders. The intrinsic component shape is a fixed parametric form; where it is wrong, the residual gate should say SHAPE, and formal errors under a mis-fit are optimistic.

3 / 13
Burst 1 of 9 · C1D1 · shared ζ

freyaCLEAN

The model citizen: the one single-component burst where every physical parameter converges away from its prior bounds.

α = 4.37 +0.02/−0.02β = 3.69 ±0.01τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 115 ±1 μsχ² C 1.36 · D 1.05t₀ ±1.0 μs (C) · ±1.3 μs (D)

Present

Single component in each band, scattering time 115 μs at 1 GHz, and the tightest fully-trusted timing of the sample: arrival times good to ~1 μs in both bands.

Explain

Data and model panels are near-identical in both bands; the residual is noise-like except faint sub-5σ mottling near +1 ms in CHIME. The auto window is ~21 ms wide in both bands (previously freya's CHIME window was clipped).

Defend

α = 4.37 and β = 3.69 land on the Kolmogorov point (α = 4.4, β = 11/3 ≈ 3.67) independently and self-consistently — the fit was free to sit anywhere in α∈[4,6], β∈[3,4] and chose turbulence theory's corner on its own.

Self-critique

χ²C = 1.36 is not 1.0: the faint CHIME structure could be a weak unmodeled sub-component; worth one C2 cross-check before the TOA enters the co-detection table. DSA ΔDM (+0.091) is still 8× the CHIME offset (+0.012).

4 / 13
Burst 2 of 9 · C2D2 · fine product

whitneyCLEAN

The burst that triggered this campaign: its second component is now modeled in both bands, and the residual is clean.

α = 4.70 +0.23/−0.21τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 36 +7/−6 μsχ² C 1.01 · D 1.04DSA comp sep 337.1 ±1.3 μst₀(D1) ±0.7 μs

Present

Two components in each band. DSA arrival times are the tightest of the whole campaign: ±0.7 and ±1.1 μs, giving a component separation of 337.1 ± 1.3 μs at DSA vs 633 ± 27 μs at CHIME.

Explain

The data panel shows the double peak clearly in both bands; the model reproduces both, including the different component ratio per band. The residual has no coherent structure — compare with the old fit, which flattened the second component into the scattering tail.

Defend

This is the direct fix for the owner-reported failure ('fits completely ignore one of the two components'). χ² ≈ 1.0 in both bands and the residual gate passes with nothing above threshold.

Self-critique

Ladder verdict (now in): a 2nd CHIME component is REAL (ΔlnZ +833/+784 across s²) and a 2nd DSA component is REAL (+17.8/+5372) — but the full C2D2 is not robustly better than C2D1 or C1D2 (rank flips with s² = prior-driven). C2D2 is defensible and its residual is clean (6.7σ, single bin), but it stays flagged rather than 'evidence-selected'. α = 4.70 also sits above Kolmogorov with the sample's shortest τ lever arm.

5 / 13
Burst 3 of 9 · C2D2

johndoeIICLEAN

First time johndoeII's second component is modeled in both bands; formally clean, with one borderline number to watch.

α = 4.06 +0.09/−0.06τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 118 +10/−9 μsχ² C 1.02 · D 1.15t₀(D1) +0.006/−0.015 msD comp sep 422 ±20 μs

Present

Two components per band: a narrow leading component (ζ ≈ 95 μs at CHIME, 66 μs at DSA) plus a broad trailing one (ζC2 ≈ 0.69 ms).

Explain

The DSA data show the sharp component followed by structure the old single-component fit averaged away; the model now carries both, and the band profiles (top strip) track the data in both bands.

Defend

χ²C = 1.02 with a matched-filter residual of only 3.0σ in CHIME — the two-component description is sufficient there by every gate.

Self-critique

The DSA matched residual is 8.2σ but diffuse (zero contiguous 5σ bins), so it does not trip ESCALATE — that is gate-consistent but worth eyes-on. The broad ζC2 = 0.69 ± 0.13 ms component partially degenerates with the scattering tail, and β = 3.94 leans toward the prior ceiling.

6 / 13
Burst 4 of 9 · C2D1

oranCLEAN

The heaviest scattering of the nine: a ~0.9 ms scattering time under a broad two-component CHIME plateau.

α = 4.04 +0.04/−0.02τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 860 +61/−64 μsχ² C 1.10 · D 0.94C comp sep 2.65 mst₀(D1) ±15 μs

Present

Two wide CHIME components (widths 0.48 and 0.57 ms, separated by 2.65 ms) under τ1GHz = 0.86 ms; single narrow DSA component timed to ±15 μs.

Explain

The CHIME data panel is a ~10 ms plateau — visually ambiguous between 'many components' and 'few components + heavy scattering'. The model chooses two + scattering and the residual panel goes flat.

Defend

χ² of 1.10/0.94 and a clean gate on a profile this messy is a genuine stress test passed: the S/N-driven resolution kept enough time structure to break the width–scattering degeneracy at the ±7% level on τ.

Self-critique

Component widths carry 20–50% errors — the C2 decomposition is soft, and the 2.65 ms separation should not be over-read. α = 4.04 sits within 2σ of the prior floor at 4.0.

7 / 13
Burst 5 of 9 · C1D1

mahiCLEAN

The lowest-S/N pair: an honest fit that reports honestly wide posteriors instead of inventing structure.

α = 4.15 +0.19/−0.10τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 231 +20/−24 μsχ² C 1.08 · D 1.07DSA matched resid 1.7σt₀(D) ±9 μs

Present

Single component per band, τ = 231 μs with 10% errors, α loosely constrained at 4.15 with the widest α posterior of the clean fits.

Explain

The DSA detection is marginal (data panel: a faint streak; matched residual after subtraction 1.7σ). CHIME carries the fit; DSA mainly anchors the arrival time and the high-frequency end of τ(ν).

Defend

Both χ² ≈ 1.07 and the residual gates are the quietest of the sample — nothing was forced. Error bars scale the way they should with S/N: compare mahi's ±9 μs DSA timing to whitney's ±0.7 μs.

Self-critique

With DSA this weak, the joint model is close to a CHIME-only fit plus a prior; the ΔDMD = −0.065 ± 0.026 offset should be treated as weakly measured, and this burst contributes little to the cross-band α test.

8 / 13
Burst 6 of 9 · C1D1 · shared ζ

chromaticaα RAILED

A high-S/N, formally clean fit whose scattering-law slope pinned itself to the prior floor.

α = 4.00 (at bound)τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 125.8 ±0.9 μsχ² C 1.16 · D 1.14ΔDM(D) +0.272 ±0.016t₀ ±3.5 μs (C)

Present

Single bright component per band, τ = 125.8 μs measured to 0.7%, residual gates clean in both bands (CHIME matched residual is 9.8σ but fully diffuse — zero contiguous 5σ bins).

Explain

The CHIME panel shows the full 400–800 MHz sweep with its scattering tail; the model tracks it closely. But α = 4.00 with zero width: the posterior is pressed against the α ∈ [4,6] prior floor, so the quoted value is a boundary, not a measurement.

Defend

Everything except α is trustworthy: windows now cover the full burst, timing is at the few-μs level, and the β rail (3.99 against the [3,4] ceiling) is the theoretically consistent partner of the α rail — see slide 12.

Self-critique

Two open items: (1) the α floor must be relaxed below 4 to find where the posterior actually peaks; (2) ΔDMD = +0.27 pc cm&supmin;³ is 26× the CHIME offset — either real frequency-dependent DM or a model artifact; it needs to be reconciled with the DM-budget lane before the TOAs are used.

9 / 13
Burst 7 of 9 · C1D1 · shared ζ

caseySHAPE 33σ

The sharpest burst of the sample, and the clearest demonstration that our component shape, not component count, is the limiting model error.

α = 4.00 (at bound)τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 19.0 ±0.1 μsχ² C 2.34 · D 1.05C matched resid 51σ6 contiguous 5σ bins

Present

Shortest scattering time (19 μs), single component per band. The CHIME fit fails χ² (2.34) with a 51σ matched residual organized as 6 contiguous 5σ bins at the burst onset.

Explain

The residual panel shows a textbook ± dipole — red immediately followed by blue across the whole CHIME band — at the rise of the burst. The model's rise is too slow, then overshoots: an attack-shape error, coherent in frequency, not a missing second burst.

Defend

The gate classified this correctly: SHAPE-MISMATCH, not ESCALATE — adding components would be the wrong fix and the pipeline refused to suggest it. The DSA band fit is simultaneously clean (χ² = 1.05), so the failure is isolated to the CHIME rise shape.

Self-critique

Under χ² = 2.34 the formal timing errors (±0.35 μs CHIME!) are fiction — casey's TOA must carry an inflated or bootstrapped error into any co-detection table. The fix is a more flexible rise (asymmetric component form), which is a model-family change, not a tuning knob. α railed at 4.0 here too.

10 / 13
Burst 8 of 9 · C1D1 · shared ζ

wilhelmSHAPE 26σ

Shape trouble in both bands: a razor-thin DSA dipole and a CHIME scattering-tail mismatch in the same fit.

α = 4.00 (at bound)τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 330 ±4 μsχ² C 1.50 · D 1.21D dipole 26σ (1 bin)C matched 14.7σ, 3 contig

Present

τ = 330 μs, single component per band. Residual verdicts: DSA SHAPE (a 26σ dipole confined to a single time bin at the burst peak), CHIME 14.7σ matched with a red-then-blue pattern along the tail.

Explain

At DSA the burst is narrower than the model can make it — the single-bin ± structure is an unresolved-width error, not new emission. At CHIME the model's tail decays slightly too fast then too slow: a τ-shape (or β-shape) mismatch stretched along 2–5 ms.

Defend

Neither band escalates — consistent with the casey lesson that the parametric shape is the bottleneck. The headline τ is still bounded usefully: the tail mismatch is at the few-percent-of-peak level on top of a χ² = 1.50 fit, and the DSA timing (±0.8 μs formal) fails soft, not silently.

Self-critique

Same caveats as casey, doubled: inflate the timing errors, and treat τ = 330 μs as carrying an un-modeled systematic from the tail shape. wilhelm was also an m > 1 offender in the scintillation lane — the two lanes' 'broad component' stories should eventually be told consistently. α railed.

11 / 13
Burst 9 of 9 · C2D1

ishaESCALATE 12σ

The failure worth studying: two degenerate parameters and a persistent escalation, all caught by the gates.

α = 4.15 +0.12/−0.09τ₁ₖₛ₠ = 324 +22/−21 μsΔDM(D) = +46.3 (!)ζ(C1) = 70 +55/−31 ms (!)D resid +12σ ESCALATE

Present

CHIME-side numbers look reasonable (χ²C = 1.00, α = 4.15, τ = 324 μs). But: component C1 fit itself as a 70 ms-wide pseudo-component absorbing the plateau; the DSA DM offset ran to +46.3 pc cm&supmin;³; and the DSA residual has a +12σ contiguous spike at +0.7 ms.

Explain

The DSA data panel explains it: heavy masking leaves usable data only in the top channels, so the narrow-band DSA component barely constrains DM — and a DM shift trades almost freely against arrival time (the +46 is that degeneracy running away, not physics). The 12σ spike is real unmodeled DSA emission.

Defend

Every pathology was flagged automatically: the ESCALATE verdict, the absurd posteriors visible at a glance. Nothing here would have entered a science table silently — and the C2D2 escalation refit was already queued and run by the pipeline.

Self-critique

The escalation did not resolve it, and the ladder verdict is now formal: C2D2 still leaves 11.1σ and its evidence is negative (ΔlnZ −10.8/−4.0 across s²) — the DSA residual is a shape/calibration artifact, not a missing component; C2D1 stays. The real fixes are a bounded ΔDMD prior and better DSA masking. isha's DSA TOA is unusable as-is and must be excluded from the co-detection table; the CHIME-side parameters need a refit with the degeneracies closed before being quoted.

The α/β corner is one story, not two bugs

All three α = 4.00 rails (casey, chromatica, wilhelm) also sit at β → 4.0. In thin-screen theory α = 2β/(β−2), so β → 4 implies α → 4: the sampler is converging to the Gaussian-screen corner of the prior box, self-consistently. The data are voting for a steeper (non-Kolmogorov) density spectrum — or exposing a shape degeneracy — and the current prior floor cuts the posterior off exactly there. Meanwhile freya sits precisely on the Kolmogorov point (α = 4.37, β = 3.69) with both parameters interior.

Timing: the point of the exercise

Where fits are clean, joint TOAs now carry formal errors of 0.7–15 μs — the precision the co-detection arrival-time comparison needs, and consistent with the 8–30× improvement estimated when the window/resolution defects were diagnosed. The fluence-weighted-centroid TOA table is the next automated step once the last three fits land.

13 / 13
Campaign self-critique & next steps

What I don't yet believe, and what happens next

  • The α ∈ [4,6] prior floor is doing science. Three of nine α's are quoted at the bound. Until a rerun with the floor relaxed (αmin ≈ 3) shows where those posteriors peak, the railed α's are lower limits, not measurements.
  • Formal errors under bad χ² are optimistic. casey (χ² = 2.34) reports ±0.35 μs timing; that is a mis-specified-model artifact. The TOA table should inflate errors by √χ² or bootstrap for any SHAPE-flagged fit.
  • Component-shape rigidity is the dominant model error — two SHAPE verdicts (casey rise, wilhelm width/tail), zero missing-component escalations among the clean nine. The fix is an asymmetric/flexible component form: a model-family decision for the owner, not a refit knob.
  • Ladder verdict (delivered): across the whole sample, no new components are warranted beyond the fits shown. whitney definitively needs a 2nd component in at least one band (C2D1 and C1D2 each beat C1D1 decisively) but the full C2D2 is prior-sensitive — defensible, flagged, not 'evidence-selected'. isha's 12σ DSA residual is formally an artifact (C2D2 doesn't absorb it and has negative ΔlnZ); C2D1 stays.
  • isha needs a targeted refit (bounded ΔDMD, DSA re-masking), not more components — the pipeline's own escalation test proved that.
  • Three fits are still running (phineas C3D3, zach C2D3, hamilton C4D1, ~5 h at review time — my 90–120 min projection was 2.5× optimistic). The postprocess watcher also died mid-campaign and was restarted; the TOA table + full triptych set generate automatically when the last fit lands.
  • Not claimed: nothing here is manuscript-ready; all numbers await the complete 12-burst set, the s² verdict, and owner ratification.

Faber2026 · joint time–frequency fits, Phase A · fits: h17 SLURM, dynesty nlive=400 · deck generated 2026-07-17
